findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Find all uses of buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate
 * @description Locate all usages of buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate in the codebase
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/find-constraint-violation-template
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-917
 */
import java

from MethodCall mc
where mc.getMethod().hasName("buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate")
select mc, "Call to buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate with argument: " + mc.getArgument(0).toString()
findings.hypothesis: Direct inspection of buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate calls to see what arguments are passed

message.text: Call to buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate with argument: getMessage(...)

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Trace what getMessage() returns in CronValidator context
 * @description Find the getMessage() call and what it receives
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/cron-validator-getmessage
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-917
 */
import java

from MethodCall mc, Method enclosing
where 
  mc.getEnclosingCallable() = enclosing and
  enclosing.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.cronutils.validation", "CronValidator") and
  mc.getMethod().hasName("getMessage")
select mc, "getMessage() call in CronValidator: qualifier=" + mc.getQualifier().toString() + ", in method=" + enclosing.getName()
findings.hypothesis: Examining what getMessage() returns in the CronValidator to understand if user cron input flows into it

message.text: getMessage() call in CronValidator: qualifier=e, in method=isValid

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Inspect CronValidator isValid method structure
 * @description Look at all method calls and variable accesses in CronValidator.isValid
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/cron-validator-structure
 * @tags security
 */
import java

from MethodCall mc
where mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName() = "isValid" and
      mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.cronutils.validation", "CronValidator")
select mc, "Call in isValid: " + mc.getMethod().getName() + "() on " + mc.getQualifier().toString() + " at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: Examining all method calls in CronValidator.isValid to understand the full code flow

message.text: Call in isValid: instanceDefinitionFor() on CronDefinitionBuilder at line 26
Call in isValid: validate() on parse(...) at line 29
Call in isValid: parse() on cronParser at line 29
Call in isValid: disableDefaultConstraintViolation() on context at line 32
Call in isValid: addConstraintViolation() on buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate(...) at line 33
Call in isValid: buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate() on context at line 33
Call in isValid: getMessage() on e at line 33

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Confirmed EL injection: cron input in exception message passed to buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate
 * @description CronValidator passes exception messages containing user cron input to buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate, which evaluates EL expressions
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/cron-el-injection-confirmed
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-917
 */
import java

// Find the catch block in CronValidator.isValid that calls buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate(e.getMessage())
from MethodCall buildCall, MethodCall getMessageCall, CatchClause cc, Method isValid
where
  // isValid method in CronValidator
  isValid.hasName("isValid") and
  isValid.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.cronutils.validation", "CronValidator") and
  
  // buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate call inside isValid
  buildCall.getEnclosingCallable() = isValid and
  buildCall.getMethod().hasName("buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate") and
  
  // The argument to buildCall is getMessage() call
  getMessageCall = buildCall.getArgument(0) and
  getMessageCall.getMethod().hasName("getMessage") and
  
  // getMessage() is called on the catch clause variable
  cc.getEnclosingCallable() = isValid and
  getMessageCall.getQualifier().(VarAccess).getVariable() = cc.getVariable().getVariable()
select buildCall, 
  "EL injection vulnerability: exception message containing user-supplied cron string is passed to buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate(), which evaluates it as an EL expression. An attacker can inject EL expressions like ${Runtime.getRuntime().exec('cmd')} in the cron string."
findings.hypothesis: Confirmed EL injection: CronValidator.isValid catches parse exceptions and passes e.getMessage() (which contains user cron string) to buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate

message.text: EL injection vulnerability: exception message containing user-supplied cron string is passed to buildConstraintViolationWithTemplate(), which evaluates it as an EL expression. An attacker can inject EL expressions like ${{Runtime.getRuntime().exec('cmd')}} in the cron string.

findings.hypothesis: Verify that the user-supplied cron string is embedded in the exception messages thrown by CronParser.parse()

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Examine exception messages containing cron user input
 * @description Find where exceptions are thrown with cron string content included in the message
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/cron-exception-with-user-input
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-917
 */
import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.TaintTracking

// Find all throw statements or exception constructors that include user input
from ThrowStmt ts, ClassInstanceExpr cie, Expr arg
where
  ts.getExpr() = cie and
  cie.getAnArgument() = arg and
  (
    cie.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().getASupertype*().hasQualifiedName("java.lang", "RuntimeException") or
    cie.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().getASupertype*().hasQualifiedName("java.lang", "Exception")
  ) and
  (
    ts.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getPackage().hasName("com.cronutils.parser") or
    ts.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getPackage().hasName("com.cronutils.model.field.expression.visitor")
  )
select ts, "Exception thrown in " + ts.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + ts.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + " with arg: " + arg.toString()
findings.hypothesis: Examining what exception messages are constructed with user cron input in parser classes
